prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS, exact four-center integrals — no density fitting), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 (cation) state used charge +1, multiplicity 2; the N+1 (anion) state used charge −1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). The finite-difference Fukui indices f+, f−, f0, and the dual descriptor were computed for all 11 atoms (N, C×5, H×5). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true; all checks passed (Σf+ = Σf− = 1.0000). No warnings reported.

Headline result: the most electrophilic atom is N (atom 1) with f+ = 0.48422572; the most nucleophilic heavy atom is C (atom 4) with f− = 0.11826260. Full per-atom Fukui indices and dual descriptors are reported in the detailed table above.
